title: Depth-recurrent transformer: shared block × 12 passes, val_bpb 1.4061, 4.39MB artifact
author: Sambhav242005
body:
## Approach
Single shared transformer block applied 12 times instead of independent layers.
Reduces unique params from ~25M → 4.7M, freeing budget to go wider (dim=768).

Key changes from baseline:
- Depth recurrence: 1 shared Block × num_passes (default 12)
- U-Net skip connections: encoder passes store activations, decoder passes consume in reverse
- x0 residual mix: original embedding injected at every pass for stability
- Wider model: MODEL_DIM=768, NUM_HEADS=12 vs baseline 512/8

## Result
- val_bpb: 1.4061 (after int8 + zlib roundtrip)
- Artifact size: 4.39MB / 16MB budget
- Parameters: 4,726,284
- Hardware: RTX 4090, ~45s training time

## Run command
MODEL_DIM=768 NUM_HEADS=12 NUM_KV_HEADS=4 NUM_PASSES=12 
MATRIX_LR=0.02 WARMDOWN_ITERS=600 MAX_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS=0 
TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS=524288 PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True
torchrun --standalone --nproc_per_node=1 train_gpt_recurrent.py
